name: Saltaire's Hot Crab Dip
directions: Preheat oven to 350.
Squeeze all juice from crab.
Mix all ingredients except crab in mixer for approx 2 minutes.
Add crab and mix for another minute.
Place the dip in a ceramic dish and bake at 350 for 7 minutes or until warm in the centre.
Serve with warm fresh bread.

name: Crab Logs
directions: Cook bacon until limp; set aside.
Combine rest of ingredients together.
Mix well and shape into logs about 1/2" long by 3/4" in diameter.
Wrap 1/2 of a bacon slice around the log and secure with toothpick.
Arrange on baking sheet and freeze.
Pack in airtight container.
Broil 4 inches from heat about 10 minutes or until golden brown turning occasionally.

name: Creamy Crab Soup
directions: Drain crab and remove bits of shell and cartilage.
Cut the legs in pieces.
Saute the onion and celery in butter for about 10 minutes.
Blend in the flour and the seasonings.
Add the milk, stirring and cooking until it is smooth and has a thick consistency.
Puree the soup in a blender.
Put the soup back in the pan and add the crab and cream.
Garnish with parsley and enjoy!

name: Crab Dip to Die For
directions: Mix ingredients in order given.
Serve in dip bowl.
Serve with vegetable sticks, mushrooms and crackers.

name: Hot Crab  Dip
directions: Preheat oven to 375°F
Combine all ingredients except almonds in a medium bowl and mix well.
Spoon into a shallow baking dish.
Bake 10 to 15 minutes until heated through. Top with toasted almonds and serve with crackers or sour dough bread slices.

name: King Crab Legs
directions: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Cut crab legs in half across width. Split open lenghtwise.
Spread garlic butter over meat and sprinkle with breadcrumbs.
Place on an ovenproof platter and pepper well.
Change oven setting to broil and place crab legs in oven.
Broil 5-6 minutes.
